name: Keith Ellison
subject: SATURDAY: Join a Resistance Summer kickoff event in Annapolis
body:
Resistance Summer starts Saturday -- make sure you're part of it
---
Derek --

We're officially launching Resistance Summer this Saturday, June 3rd, and I want you to be part of it.

**Can you attend a Resistance Summer house party in Annapolis on Saturday?** I'll be hosting a livestream to kick off our nationwide summer of organizing.

Here are the details:

**Resistance & Renewal with the Maryland Democratic Party**
**When:** Saturday, June 3rd at 1:30 pm EDT
**Where:** Lawyers’ Mall
100 State Circle
Annapolis, MD 21401

**[RSVP](http://my.democrats.org/page/event/detail/4vg4q)**
All summer, we're going to be organizing in communities like yours -- training volunteers, talking to our neighbors about our values and vision for the future, and getting out the vote for Democrats in local elections this year.

That's how we'll turn the energy and passion of the resistance into victories at the ballot box. We're standing up and fighting for Democrats who will stop Donald Trump's agenda in its tracks.

**[So take the first step, Derek. Join a Resistance Summer kickoff event in Annapolis on Saturday.](http://my.democrats.org/page/event/detail/4vg4q)**

When Democrats show up, we win. Thanks for being part of this -- we're just getting started.

Keith

Keith Ellison
Deputy Chair
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. name: Patrick Murphy
subject: Quick update for you
body:
| |
---
| | [](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2ed8/738133e4/2ceef28d/7f85defd/2431237233/VEsH/ "https://www.murphyforflorida.com")
---
| Friends -- With the U.S. now shamefully withdrawing from the Paris climate agreement, we can't just be concerned with how we are doing under the chaotic and disastrous Trump administration -- we need to be concerned with what we're doing. So I thought I would give you a quick update on what I have been doing in recent weeks. Here's a quick snapshot for you:
* By now, you know I've relaunched my political action committee, aimed at electing a new Congress who will put country ahead of party. The first candidate I helped was Democrat Jon Ossoff in the Georgia special election coming up this month.
* Last week, I authored an op-ed for the Palm Beach Post on what happens when intelligence leaks come from the top:

_We need an independent investigation into the Russia matter once and for all, and allow the chips to fall where they may; the naming of former FBI Director Robert Mueller as Special Counsel into the matter is a good start. President Trump once warned America that if his opponent had won the election, she'd be a distraction under constant investigation. Clearly, the reverse is true._ [Check out the rest here.](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2ed8/738133e4/2ceef28d/7f85defe/2431237233/VEsE/ "http://www.palmbeachpost.com/news/opinion/point-view-when-leaks-come-from-the-top/ovBcRDXjm5yeXJOMJbIsSL/")
* I sat down with WTVT in Tampa to talk about the 2016 election and what lessons we can learn. [Watch it here.](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2ed8/738133e4/2ceef28d/7f85deff/2431237233/VEsF/ "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KRyviNwZCGI")
* I spoke with college students at the University of Miami and middle schoolers at Palmer Trinity (both my alma maters) about how politics shaped my life -- and listened to how it's affecting theirs.

I know you're taking action too -- thank you for stepping up and fighting for what's right. I will continue being an active voice for the issues I care about and the state and country I love. By holding fast to our ideals of equality and justice, we will build a better future for everyone. Thank you, Patrick [CONTRIBUTE](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2ed8/738133e4/2ceef28d/7f85def8/2431237233/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/170601pem") |
Paid for by PEM PAC | [http://www.pempac.com](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2ed8/738133e4/2ceef28d/7f85def9/2431237233/VEsD/ "http://www.pempac.com") | Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)
[Click here](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2ed8/738133e4/2ceef28d/7f85defa/2431237233/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://murphyforflorida.com/subscriptions/?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com") to unsubscribe

Contributions or gifts to PEM PAC are not tax deductible.

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: Resistance Summer starts tomorrow - make sure you're part of it
body:
You don't want to miss the Resistance Summer kickoff tomorrow Derek --

As soon as the election results came in last November, Democrats knew we were going to be the opposition party to the Trump administration and Republican Congress.

But it's not enough to be an opposition party. We have to be the proposition party -- proposing our vision of the future so that, as the American people reject what they see in front of them, they can turn to us.

That's why I'm so excited about Resistance Summer, Derek. It's all about strengthening and connecting our communities so we can resist Donald Trump's agenda and spread the word about the progressive values Democrats are fighting for.

**Make sure you're part of this summer of organizing -- it all starts tomorrow, June 3rd. RSVP for a Resistance Summer kickoff event in your community.**

Here are the details:

**Resistance Summer Kick-Off Watch Party**
**When:** Saturday, June 3rd at 4:30 pm EDT
**Where:** Malley House
4217 Lenore Lane NW
Washington, DC 20008

**[RSVP](http://my.democrats.org/page/event/detail/4vgqd)**
Thanks -- now let's get ready to organize and win.

Pramila

Pramila Jayapal
U.S. Representative, Washington

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Science
body:
#
Team --
Let me be clear -- President Trump's excuse for leaving the Paris Accord is unquestionably wrong. Not only is he placing the fate of our planet in jeopardy, but he is doing so under the guise that it will somehow be good for jobs and the economy.
**But in reality, Florida is the number one state in the nation in total business and property value at risk from climate change. We are the epicenter of climate change -- and yet, our state government employees aren 't allowed to use the term 'climate change.' In other words, we know we have problem, but we're not allowed to talk about it.**
When I'm Governor, that will change. Not only will we lift the ban on the term 'climate change,' we're going to join California, New York and Washington in their state-based climate alliance to uphold and implement the Paris Climate Accord.
We're going to create jobs in clean energy, just like we're already doing in Tallahassee, by investing in the production of solar energy, in creating more energy efficient technologies, and by supporting our colleges and universities to lead in research and development around addressing sea level rise. And those are jobs you can't outsource.
[**If you have had enough of climate deniers endangering our state, then chip in now to elect a Governor of Florida who believes in climate change >>**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3336/9e00690/4a49af96/240373f9/3805992747/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170602-climate")
With your help, we will overcome President Trump's terrible decisions on the state level, with policy based in science and fact.
Bring it home,
Andrew
**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3336/9e00690/4a49af96/240373f9/3805992747/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170602-climate")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3336/9e00690/4a49af96/240373f8/3805992747/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). name: New Hampshire Republican Party
subject: Your Weekly NH GOP Newsletter
body:
| |
---
|
**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, June 2nd |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,
Last night I attended the New Boston Republican Town Committee’s June meeting. It was wonderful to be able to share with the folks there the successes we had yesterday in the House. The passage of SB3, SB8, and SB66 are perfect examples of the Republican legislature working together to push forward common sense legislation that is good for New Hampshire.
“[ _**SB3**_](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbT0pW52ma3LTMrghVm-2FI6cWOrzttPYXIL8ZgL2Ta1Vr73-2F9HSATFLrM8Yz97datyyXPyj4dngtqVB-2FNa4XD4XRE-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30Ml4oNYkKH307otaOvL6RRqRnuTfAzjYvnUufipwjE1wmgUFgkTuubTfPws4VeL4JDleZm-2Bo9TUo1BfRLsk3AQY2gYFJjXlaNTRpjS-2BDUsOqbZdU-2BVp7BNn9AnQDCfLD7RVl2a0YSm9jkBMzrBnyhH4lgZKWUF3EhHK3QVxdYoLkXp5lmB7fMGzNODscIpdmPIYHBG-2FoXbPF7oNQvlCZ0ZOp5rOdqQtAbg7ws95398PGEXHFXdDRU-2BKRHThaiZkOWb8RGUq6rFoj3JOOvNQTJ-2BIw-3D-3D) holds all residents to an equal standard and makes sure that every duly qualified voter who shows up to the polls has the opportunity to vote,” said House Majority Leader Dick Hinch (R-Merrimack). The NHGOP applauds Senator Birdsell, Representative Barbara Griffin, and Representative Yvonne Dean-Bailey for their yeoman’s work on this bill.
[_**SB8**_](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbT0pW52ma3LTMrghVm-2FI6cWOrzttPYXIL8ZgL2Ta1Vr73-2F9HSATFLrM8Yz97datyyXPyj4dngtqVB-2FNa4XD4XRE-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30Ml4oNYkKH307otaOvL6RRqRnuTfAzjYvnUufipwjE1wmgUFgkTuubTfPws4VeL4JDleZm-2Bo9TUo1BfRLsk3AQY0N3KSwiUQukOZP5FVxMrYIe65DI3ycHbJ2Y58kVe4wuK5L4Mvh5kGxDtACi8PIOFzZxghCrcRkPdRJwC6f7UJTLaa9ZmJXsFpPxHJFf7LC-2B7K6VSjbXUV2z2eDq6VQdftA6V1Qf3-2BgpskFqjtj-2B0SlLXQ-2BhyNI9yRggo61LeWQYNJHxLumuwq-2F-2FTivw420Wkw-3D-3D) will ...”allow students living in a town without a public school to have the opportunity to attend a public or private accredited school of their choosing in another community,” said Senator Ruth Ward (R-Stoddard), the prime sponsor of the bill. Expanding school choice is a good thing!
[_**SB66**_](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbT0pW52ma3LTMrghVm-2FI6cWOrzttPYXIL8ZgL2Ta1Vr73-2F9HSATFLrM8Yz97datyyXPyj4dngtqVB-2FNa4XD4XRE-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30Ml4oNYkKH307otaOvL6RRqRnuTfAzjYvnUufipwjE1wmgUFgkTuubTfPws4VeL4JDleZm-2Bo9TUo1BfRLsk3AQY1IjsXJvmdd5jCHawty0g6HGU3GZ9G7KoImXGB-2BovnuJ37Z85tePRymViLSPNff7OWY7Agt0BpSgQxlGbjKnACMNSxPerBBIjbf5ub1WfWU2lPwzvj4uXXjduYR3w8qWuH0jNkmGMjfE0NoC3riE-2FH-2Fsrz-2B95QqIP-2BOFbC8XzPQaWPKuhKrlvBl78s29-2BeU5Ig-3D-3D) “...would give women a voice and provide the necessary closure and justice should their choice to carry a pregnancy to term be taken away,” said Sen. Regina Birdsell (R-Hampstead), prime sponsor of the bill.
Also this week, the [_**Senate worked late into the night to pass the State budget for Fiscal Year 2018-2019**_](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbY1qiyT3HuRDH6pVWPAnYR6FcHAlAfrDanC8C-2BXpcAouGk8zjjlhPOCJQlttgyDNtM0PVmTuC9l3bAmmvPF6V0fwedYSy1wZnsvu42MzNhi-2FiQy5Oqdg4tuLtHamvdYNFkTI0c-2BApjuqx78SyiT2OEI-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30Ml4oNYkKH307otaOvL6RRqRnuTfAzjYvnUufipwjE1wmgUFgkTuubTfPws4VeL4JDleZm-2Bo9TUo1BfRLsk3AQYzmlwISut6jqQ4fMYhUnksllMZlFFjZqlTVfRCQLgT6w1DIWc9Or9nYm-2Bmlrpzy06rY7YFX-2BcHHtq0qxSuDnqYx2hHHqVGS6YU7MFC8ll-2FHZJip2g2wTJh5kkUy00e5J7cTrECubujdd7e1qAFZbyQ1XLDlEac3bO6HUrN6e-2F1Mf0SHAo-2Fn4HwXvuYD5s2moIg-3D-3D), incorporating much of the good work done in the House. This budget includes business tax relief, mental health and substance abuse funding among other important issues. Said Finance Chair Gary Daniels, ...”I’d like to recognize the House’s work on the budget and Governor Sununu’s leadership and vision for the state of NH for the next two years. We were able to include many of the policies that were part of the House and Governor’s budgets.”
All-in-all a very good week for Republicans and especially for the citizens of New Hampshire!
Sincerely, _Jeanie_
|
---
---

| **Governor Chris Sununu Statement on SB 3**
**Concord, NH –** Today following the House passage of SB 3, relative to domicile for voting purposes, Governor Chris Sununu issued the following statement:
“This legislation helps protect the integrity of New Hampshire’s electoral process. As host of the First in the Nation primary, New Hampshire has the obligation to ensure our system is beyond reproach. This bill does exactly that and as such, I support SB 3 and commend the House of Representatives for their actions today.”

* * *

**SB 66, Including a fetus in the definition of "another"** **for purposes of certain criminal offenses**
“Today, the House of Representatives passed a critical fix that allows prosecution of individuals who criminally end the life of a viable unborn baby. It provides security and addresses injustice for women who may have been in abusive situations that resulted in the death of a fetus.”

* * *

**SB 8, Relative to school attendance in towns with no public schools.**
“I look forward to signing SB 8 and fulfilling a commitment I made to the citizens of our state. Parents, children, and school districts must have the ability to choose the education path that is best suited for them and this legislation will ensure that small school districts have flexibility in choosing that path. This bill will not only resolve Croydon’s situation but will also benefit more than a dozen small districts across the state.”

|
| **Legislative Highlights** |
---|---|---
---
| _**House Republicans**_
**Majority Leader Applauds House Passage of SB3** **** Today, the House passed SB3, relative to domicile for voting purposes. The bill as amended by the House eliminates the domicile loophole that allows voters to register without proving their domicile while also shifting the responsibility of proving domicile on to the voter. The bill also creates a clear process for verifying an individual's domicile and clarifies how municipal and state officials should address questions of domicile. The bill passed the House today by a 191-162 vote.

House Majority Leader Richard “Dick” Hinch (R-Merrimack) issued the following statement:
“I applaud the hard work of our Election Law experts in the House and Senate and am pleased to see SB3 pass the House. New Hampshire holds more local and state elections than any other state in the nation, with numerous outcomes being decided by single digit margins. SB3 will help maintain confidence in our elections by eliminating drive-by voting and removing the domicile loophole by making domicile a verifiable act.”
“SB3 holds all residents to an equal standard and makes sure that every duly qualified voter who shows up to the polls has the opportunity to vote.”
“I look forward to getting this important legislation to the Governor's desk as quickly as possible.”

* * *
**Majority Leader Responds to Passage of SB8**
House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack) released the following statement in response to the House passed SB8, relative to school attendance in towns with no public schools. SB8 passed the House on a bipartisan vote of 210-147.
House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack):
“The passage of SB8 is an important step forward in school choice. This bill will give school boards and parents more choices in order to send their students to the best possible school. SB8 guarantees all students will have the chance to receive an adequate education, whether it’s a public school or a non-sectarian private school. We applaud the committee’s hard work on this important piece of legislation, and we look forward to it getting to the Governor’s desk.” |

| _**Senate Republicans**_
**Senate passes state budget for Fiscal Year 2018-19** _**Includes business tax relief, mental health and substance abuse funding**_
**Concord, NH -** Today, the Senate passed HB 144 and HB 517, the bills which make up the Fiscal Year 2018-19 state budget.
“I would like to thank the Senate for their support of the Fiscal Year 2018-19 budget and for the work of the SenateFinance Committee and especially Senator Gary Daniels for working with the House, Senate and Governor to produce a budget that lives within our means and works for the state of New Hampshire,” said Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem). “This budget balances the needs of our citizens while also growing our economy and creating good jobs for New Hampshire families without raising or creating any new taxes or fees. This budget provides funding to tackle the heroin crisis, increases support for families with a disabled child and adds beds for mental health patients, among other key priorities that will effectively help and care for our friends and neighbors.
“We also took steps to grow our state’s Rainy Day Fund to $100 million, creating a strong economic foundation for our state while cutting the business profits tax and business enterprise tax ensuring future job creation and that New Hampshire remains a competitive place for small businesses. These changes and more will continue to make New Hampshire a great place to live for working families,” added Morse.
“I am pleased that the Senate passed the FY 18-19 budget for the State of New Hampshire. The Finance Committee spent a number of weeks listening to the needs of each department, organization and citizen whose daily life would be affected by the outcome of this budget,” said Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels (R-Milford). “I’d like to also recognize the House’s work on the budget and Governor Sununu’s leadership and vision for the state of New Hampshire for the next two years. We were able to include many of the policies that were part of the House and Governor’s budgets.”
“We addressed our state’s most critical needs in this budget, including programs to benefit families with a disabled child, adding 60 new beds and community treatment options to relieve the growing mental health problem while also making critical changes to the leadership, oversight and staffing levels at the Division of Children, Youth and Families so that children in abusive homes have the protection they deserve,” said Daniels. “Following Governor Sununu’s guidance, we adopted a plan to develop a youth substance abuse treatment center at the Sununu Youth Services Center using $2 million in unspent funds in the Governor’s commission on Alcohol and Substance Abuse. By renovating a wing of the Sununu Center, we will be able to help our young people overcome their addiction early on and pave a positive path forward. We also agreed to double the funding for the Governor’s Commission on Alcohol and Substance Abuse Prevention, Treatment and Recovery in this budget bringing it to $14 million, up from $7 million. As of last Friday, the Commission had $4.3 million in unspent funds for the fiscal year ending June 30. Frankly, this is a tremendous disappointment in the middle of a heroin crisis and we need to make sure that these resources are being used in our communities building on the positive programs created to combat all sides of the heroin crisis.”
“The budget that passed the Senate today also includes additional business tax cuts and increases our small businesses’ ability to make capital investments. This allows businesses in New Hampshire to create good paying jobs and grow their business, keeping our state competitive with other states in New England and nationally,” added Daniels.
“New Hampshire is facing a high tech workforce shortage and it is prudent that we create opportunities for students to become engaged in science, technology, engineering and math [STEM] fields through programs like FIRST Robotics,” said Senator John Reagan (R-Deerfield). “This budget provides resources that would bring FIRST Robotics into every elementary school in the state which can play a critical role in building a student’s interest in STEM and provide skills for real world application. This program also encourages local businesses to step up and become involved in our children’s education that will create a stronger skilled workforce in the future.”
“I support the FY 2018-19 budget that passed the Senate today because it not only lives within our means, only increasing general fund spending by 1.4 percent in 2018 and 1.1 percent in 2019, but because of the important priorities it achieves in the next two years for the citizens and small businesses in our state. This budget reduces tax burdens on our small businesses, which will support the 12.1 percent business tax revenue above projections in 2017, while maintaining one of the lowest unemployment rates in the nation and one of the fastest growing economies in the country,” said Senator Bob Giuda (R-Warren). “This improves our ability to be competitive in business with other states in New England and protects the attractive and competitive climate businesses are looking for.”
“This budget also builds the Rainy Day Fund up to $100 million which is simply responsible budgeting that will strengthen our state’s economic footing and build on New Hampshire’s bond rating,” added Giuda.

This week's spotlight shines on State Reps Yvonne Dean-Bailey & Barbara Griffin! Yvonne and Barbara, along with Senator Regina Birdsell, spearheaded the House efforts to pass SB3, relative to closing the domicile loophole in New Hampshire. They have worked tirelessly to promote this bill and worked with their colleagues in the House to inform Granite Staters that this bill helps and does not hurt election integrity. We thank them both for their continued service and dedication to the Party and for promoting our core principles. New Hampshire is lucky to have them! Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? | | | Governor's Corner | ---|---|--- | Governor Chris Sununu Statement on SB 3 Concord, NH – Today following the House passage of SB 3, relative to domicile for voting purposes, Governor Chris Sununu issued the following statement: “This legislation helps protect the integrity of New Hampshire’s electoral process. As host of the First in the Nation primary, New Hampshire has the obligation to ensure our system is beyond reproach. This bill does exactly that and as such, I support SB 3 and commend the House of Representatives for their actions today.” SB 66, Including a fetus in the definition of "another" for purposes of certain criminal offenses “Today, the House of Representatives passed a critical fix that allows prosecution of individuals who criminally end the life of a viable unborn baby. It provides security and addresses injustice for women who may have been in abusive situations that resulted in the death of a fetus.” SB 8, Relative to school attendance in towns with no public schools. “I look forward to signing SB 8 and fulfilling a commitment I made to the citizens of our state. Parents, children, and school districts must have the ability to choose the education path that is best suited for them and this legislation will ensure that small school districts have flexibility in choosing that path. This bill will not only resolve Croydon’s situation but will also benefit more than a dozen small districts across the state.” | | Legislative Highlights | ---|---|--- | House Republicans Majority Leader Applauds House Passage of SB3 ** Today, the House passed SB3, relative to domicile for voting purposes. The bill as amended by the House eliminates the domicile loophole that allows voters to register without proving their domicile while also shifting the responsibility of proving domicile on to the voter. The bill also creates a clear process for verifying an individual's domicile and clarifies how municipal and state officials should address questions of domicile. The bill passed the House today by a 191-162 vote. House Majority Leader Richard “Dick” Hinch (R-Merrimack) issued the following statement: “I applaud the hard work of our Election Law experts in the House and Senate and am pleased to see SB3 pass the House. New Hampshire holds more local and state elections than any other state in the nation, with numerous outcomes being decided by single digit margins. SB3 will help maintain confidence in our elections by eliminating drive-by voting and removing the domicile loophole by making domicile a verifiable act.” “SB3 holds all residents to an equal standard and makes sure that every duly qualified voter who shows up to the polls has the opportunity to vote.” “I look forward to getting this important legislation to the Governor's desk as quickly as possible.” Majority Leader Responds to Passage of SB8 House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack) released the following statement in response to the House passed SB8, relative to school attendance in towns with no public schools. SB8 passed the House on a bipartisan vote of 210-147. House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack): “The passage of SB8 is an important step forward in school choice. This bill will give school boards and parents more choices in order to send their students to the best possible school. SB8 guarantees all students will have the chance to receive an adequate education, whether it’s a public school or a non-sectarian private school. We applaud the committee’s hard work on this important piece of legislation, and we look forward to it getting to the Governor’s desk.” | | Senate Republicans Senate passes state budget for Fiscal Year 2018-19 Includes business tax relief, mental health and substance abuse funding Concord, NH - Today, the Senate passed HB 144 and HB 517, the bills which make up the Fiscal Year 2018-19 state budget. “I would like to thank the Senate for their support of the Fiscal Year 2018-19 budget and for the work of the SenateFinance Committee and especially Senator Gary Daniels for working with the House, Senate and Governor to produce a budget that lives within our means and works for the state of New Hampshire,” said Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem). “This budget balances the needs of our citizens while also growing our economy and creating good jobs for New Hampshire families without raising or creating any new taxes or fees. This budget provides funding to tackle the heroin crisis, increases support for families with a disabled child and adds beds for mental health patients, among other key priorities that will effectively help and care for our friends and neighbors. “We also took steps to grow our state’s Rainy Day Fund to $100 million, creating a strong economic foundation for our state while cutting the business profits tax and business enterprise tax ensuring future job creation and that New Hampshire remains a competitive place for small businesses. These changes and more will continue to make New Hampshire a great place to live for working families,” added Morse. “I am pleased that the Senate passed the FY 18-19 budget for the State of New Hampshire. The Finance Committee spent a number of weeks listening to the needs of each department, organization and citizen whose daily life would be affected by the outcome of this budget,” said Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels (R-Milford). “I’d like to also recognize the House’s work on the budget and Governor Sununu’s leadership and vision for the state of New Hampshire for the next two years. We were able to include many of the policies that were part of the House and Governor’s budgets.” “We addressed our state’s most critical needs in this budget, including programs to benefit families with a disabled child, adding 60 new beds and community treatment options to relieve the growing mental health problem while also making critical changes to the leadership, oversight and staffing levels at the Division of Children, Youth and Families so that children in abusive homes have the protection they deserve,” said Daniels. “Following Governor Sununu’s guidance, we adopted a plan to develop a youth substance abuse treatment center at the Sununu Youth Services Center using $2 million in unspent funds in the Governor’s commission on Alcohol and Substance Abuse. By renovating a wing of the Sununu Center, we will be able to help our young people overcome their addiction early on and pave a positive path forward. We also agreed to double the funding for the Governor’s Commission on Alcohol and Substance Abuse Prevention, Treatment and Recovery in this budget bringing it to $14 million, up from $7 million. As of last Friday, the Commission had $4.3 million in unspent funds for the fiscal year ending June 30. Frankly, this is a tremendous disappointment in the middle of a heroin crisis and we need to make sure that these resources are being used in our communities building on the positive programs created to combat all sides of the heroin crisis.” “The budget that passed the Senate today also includes additional business tax cuts and increases our small businesses’ ability to make capital investments. This allows businesses in New Hampshire to create good paying jobs and grow their business, keeping our state competitive with other states in New England and nationally,” added Daniels. “New Hampshire is facing a high tech workforce shortage and it is prudent that we create opportunities for students to become engaged in science, technology, engineering and math [STEM] fields through programs like FIRST Robotics,” said Senator John Reagan (R-Deerfield). “This budget provides resources that would bring FIRST Robotics into every elementary school in the state which can play a critical role in building a student’s interest in STEM and provide skills for real world application. This program also encourages local businesses to step up and become involved in our children’s education that will create a stronger skilled workforce in the future.” “I support the FY 2018-19 budget that passed the Senate today because it not only lives within our means, only increasing general fund spending by 1.4 percent in 2018 and 1.1 percent in 2019, but because of the important priorities it achieves in the next two years for the citizens and small businesses in our state. This budget reduces tax burdens on our small businesses, which will support the 12.1 percent business tax revenue above projections in 2017, while maintaining one of the lowest unemployment rates in the nation and one of the fastest growing economies in the country,” said Senator Bob Giuda (R-Warren). “This improves our ability to be competitive in business with other states in New England and protects the attractive and competitive climate businesses are looking for.” “This budget also builds the Rainy Day Fund up to $100 million which is simply responsible budgeting that will strengthen our state’s economic footing and build on New Hampshire’s bond rating,” added Giuda. | | This Week's Best Tweets! | ---|---|--- | Tweet With Us! | | ---|---|--- | Connect with your Republican Leaders! | ---|---|--- | | | | | Speaker of the House Shawn Jasper | | | Senate President Chuck Morse | | | Executive Council | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | | | Governor Chris Sununu | | | Chairman Forrester & The NH GOP | ---|---|--- | | Web - "LIKE" on Facebook \- "FOLLOW" on Twitter - | | | Web - Facebook \- Twitter - Chairman Jeanie Forrester "LIKE" on Facebook - "FOLLOW" on Twitter - "FOLLOW" The Staff on Twitter Finance Director - Political Director - | | 10 Water Street, Concord, NH 03301 Phone: (603) 225-9341 | Email: info@nhgop.org | | | ---|---|--- | | mailto:? | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican Party of New Hampshire · 10 Water Street · Concord · New Hampshire · 03301 \--UNSUBSCRIBE_ALT--

**_Bringing Jobs to America & Indiana_**
Jobs were again the focus of this week - both in Indiana and across the country. Here at home, Governor **Eric Holcomb** [announced plans to travel](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0265489da3&e=1acd9bb3d6) to Hungary and France on his first international trade mission since taking office. You'll recall he made a trip across the pond even before being sworn in as governor to jump start the jobs discussion and he's constantly in conversations with international businesses looking to relocate to low-cost, business-friendly states like Indiana. Nationally, President **Donald Trump** and Vice President **Mike Pence** kept their campaign promise to American workers when [they announced](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=bf9038d03c&e=1acd9bb3d6) the United States will officially part ways with the Paris Climate Accord, a deal that President Barack Obama unilaterally agreed to without seeking the consent of the U.S. Senate. Staying in this deal would have cost our country over 6 million jobs - likely many coming right here in Indiana.

\- Kyle

| Governor Holcomb and First Lady Janet Holcomb welcomed a few familiar faces home to Indiana during race weekend - Vice President Mike Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence! In case you missed it, the vice president's motorcade [took a little trip](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=26bfafb148&e=1acd9bb3d6) around the famed Indianapolis Motor Speedway oval, but at much lower speeds than most cars driving that track!

The governor also, together with Treasurer **Kelly Mitchell** and Superintendent of Public Instruction Dr. **Jennifer McCormick** , welcomed the 2017 Governor's STEM Team award winners to the Governor's Office to recognize their amazing achievements in science, technology, engineering and math. Learn more about them [here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a9862044b6&e=1acd9bb3d6).

_**The Becky Skillman Leadership Institute Series with Keynote Speaker Karen Pence**_
The 5th Annual Becky Skillman Leadership Institute Series will take place Friday, June 16th at the Boys and Girls Club of Lawrence County in Bedford. Hear from Indiana's very own, Second Lady **Karen Pence** , as well as Major General **Courtney P. Carr** , Indiana's Adjutant General, country singer **Clayton Anderson** , Butler Women's Basketball Coach **Kurt Godlevski** and Indiana University Football Coach **Tom Allen** at this engaging event. More info available [here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4c2146d7f6&e=1acd9bb3d6).

The Becky Skillman Leadership Institute was started to encourage leaders to aim higher, inspire others, maximize their potential, and to express leadership in it's truest form: service.

| The media has no interest in telling you this, but here are a few important facts regarding energy in the United States and the Paris Climate Accord:
* The U.S. is _**the**_ leader in clean energy. You can see this right here in Indiana with wind farms and other innovative forms of energy popping up all the time - in addition to _**clean**_ coal!
* In the last 10 years, carbon emissions declined by 12.5 percent in the U.S. _**without**_ the Paris Climate Accord. Think about that: we didn't need to be part of some non-binding global compact to reduce emissions.
* The Paris Climate Accord could cost our economy millions of jobs over the next several years. While some might prefer seeing _**China**_ become the world's economic leader, we think it's most important to protect Hoosier and American jobs!

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | Bringing Jobs to America & Indiana Jobs were again the focus of this week - both in Indiana and across the country. Here at home, Governor Eric Holcomb to Hungary and France on his first international trade mission since taking office. You'll recall he made a trip across the pond even before being sworn in as governor to jump start the jobs discussion and he's constantly in conversations with international businesses looking to relocate to low-cost, business-friendly states like Indiana. Nationally, President Donald Trump and Vice President Mike Pence kept their campaign promise to American workers when the United States will officially part ways with the Paris Climate Accord, a deal that President Barack Obama unilaterally agreed to without seeking the consent of the U.S. Senate. Staying in this deal would have cost our country over 6 million jobs - likely many coming right here in Indiana. \- Kyle | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | Governor Holcomb and First Lady Janet Holcomb welcomed a few familiar faces home to Indiana during race weekend - Vice President Mike Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence! In case you missed it, the vice president's motorcade around the famed Indianapolis Motor Speedway oval, but at much lower speeds than most cars driving that track! The governor also, together with Treasurer Kelly Mitchell and Superintendent of Public Instruction Dr. Jennifer McCormick , welcomed the 2017 Governor's STEM Team award winners to the Governor's Office to recognize their amazing achievements in science, technology, engineering and math. Learn more about them . | | | The Becky Skillman Leadership Institute Series with Keynote Speaker Karen Pence The 5th Annual Becky Skillman Leadership Institute Series will take place Friday, June 16th at the Boys and Girls Club of Lawrence County in Bedford. Hear from Indiana's very own, Second Lady Karen Pence , as well as Major General Courtney P. Carr , Indiana's Adjutant General, country singer Clayton Anderson , Butler Women's Basketball Coach Kurt Godlevski and Indiana University Football Coach Tom Allen at this engaging event. More info available . The Becky Skillman Leadership Institute was started to encourage leaders to aim higher, inspire others, maximize their potential, and to express leadership in it's truest form: service. | | | | The media has no interest in telling you this, but here are a few important facts regarding energy in the United States and the Paris Climate Accord: The U.S. is the leader in clean energy. You can see this right here in Indiana with wind farms and other innovative forms of energy popping up all the time - in addition to clean coal! In the last 10 years, carbon emissions declined by 12.5 percent in the U.S. without the Paris Climate Accord. Think about that: we didn't need to be part of some non-binding global compact to reduce emissions. The Paris Climate Accord could cost our economy millions of jobs over the next several years. While some might prefer seeing China become the world's economic leader, we think it's most important to protect Hoosier and American jobs! | | | Thank you to Senator Todd Young for stopping by Headquarters this week to meet with our 2017 Summer Intern Class!
